recommendations.problem: Canary timeout set too close to normal execution duration, causing dry run to fail on Lambda cold start

solution.command:
# Via AWS Console:
# 1. Navigate to CloudWatch Synthetics console
# 2. Select your canary
# 3. Go to the "Monitoring" tab
# 4. Scroll to the "Lambda metrics for selected time range" section
# 5. Find the "Duration" graph - compare the spike during a dry run vs normal runs
#    (dry run data points will be noticeably higher due to cold start)
solution.description: Check Lambda Duration metrics to understand the gap between warm and cold start execution times
solution.expected_outcome: Identifies the maximum Lambda duration including cold start to set an appropriate timeout
solution.step: Measure normal execution duration and cold start overhead

solution.command:
# Via AWS Console:
# 1. Navigate to CloudWatch Synthetics console
# 2. Select your canary
# 3. Click "Actions" > "Edit"
# 4. Under "Canary settings", increase the timeout value
#    (recommended: normal duration + 10-15 seconds for cold start buffer)
# 5. Save changes and re-run the dry run to verify

# Via AWS CLI:
aws synthetics update-canary \
  --name <canary-name> \
  --region <canary-region> \
  --run-config TimeoutInSeconds=<new-timeout>
solution.description: Set canary timeout to at least (max normal duration + cold start overhead + buffer)
solution.expected_outcome: Dry run completes successfully without timeout, console no longer shows 'Update Dry Run Failed'

recommendations.title: Increase canary timeout to account for dry run cold start overhead

root_cause: Dry runs always execute on a fresh Lambda function version, forcing a cold start. During cold start, the runtime decompresses the Chromium browser binary into /tmp, adding ~8-9 seconds of overhead. If the canary timeout is set close to the warm-start execution duration, the cold start overhead pushes total Lambda duration past the timeout. The canary script completes and emits metrics (SuccessPercentDryRun = 100%), but Lambda times out before returning the result to the Synthetics service, so the dry run is marked as failed. This is expected behavior, not a bug.
symptoms: Console shows 'Update Dry Run Failed' but SuccessPercentDryRun metric is 100%
Error: 'No Test Result Returned. Connection timed out after Xms'
Canary logs show 'End Canary. Result: PASSED' with no errors
Lambda Duration during dry run is significantly higher than normal runs
Dry run availability tab shows purple dot at 0% despite successful script execution
Issue only occurs during dry run, not regular canary runs
title: Update Dry Run Failed despite SuccessPercentDryRun metric showing 100%
